mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings prospects, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive equity returns over the next month during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of greater than 0.5% fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a frequency exceeding 60% in early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor market expansion and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 240.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly real personal spending confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to generate a positive return frequency above 57% over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes in late-cycle periods yield positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of instances.
